name: Ceylonese Chicken Curry
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut 1 onion into fourths.
2. In a blender or food processor, process onion, ginger, and garlic until very finely chopped.
3. Heat 3 tablespoons of oil in a saucepan.
4. Add the onion mixture and curry powder to the saucepan and fry for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Cut chicken into bite-size cubes and add to the onion mixture. Fry until the chicken is seared.
6. Stir in flour and cook for 1 minute.
7. Stir in stock and bring to a boil.
8. Cover and simmer gently for 15 minutes.
9. Meanwhile, peel remaining onions and separate them into rings.
10. Heat remaining oil in a skillet.
11. Add celery, onion rings, bell pepper, cumin seeds, and mushrooms (if desired) to the skillet and fry gently for 4 minutes.
12. Add the vegetable mixture to the chicken mixture and cook for an additional 15 minutes.
13. Stir in creamed coconut.
14. Add tomatoes and heat through.
15. Garnish with shredded coconut and chervil sprigs, if desired, and serve hot.
